notes: With this latest Proton Beta the game runs perfectly out of the box, including save of settings and game progress. Tested with XBox 360 and Steam Controller so far, no tweaks required.
title: Project CARS

notes:
Had to fall back to DirectX 9 because of buggy reflections in the mirrors. DirectX9 performance is significantly lower than DirectX11 performance, so keep that in mind.

After 10 minutes, the game blackscreens all monitors (two, in my case), but continues to run (you can hear that the game is still reacting to your inputs). Alt-tabbing out and back in makes the game reappear.

Aside from these (somewhat major) issues, the game runs as expected.
title: Project CARS

notes: seems to run perfectly out of the box with no performance issues

notes:
Runs perfectly at excellent performance, but MSAA breaks reflections, you should therefore disable it.
Singleplayer and Multiplayer tested; Used Steam Controller.

notes: Installed, run'd, got to the main menu, did a complete single player race, no issues at all, no visible graphical issues, looks platinum to me.

title: Project CARS

notes: Works perfectly without tweaks on this version (4.2-2), and on the previous version (3.16-8). Playing on generic XBox One controller. Vibration works. Haven't tested online multiplayer. Alt+Tab causes crashes the game. However, this does not affect the normal running of the game, so my Platinum rating stands. 9 hours logged as of this review, and no crashes under normal gameplay conditions.
title: Project CARS

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box without any tweaks required. Though i don't have any reference for this game on Windows, i would say that it runs great for this particular setup.
title: Project CARS

notes:
To be honest I have not tested every aspect of the game. Just tested the game if it runs and as per other users Alt+Tab causes crashes. Apparently, alt+tab crashes the game in Windows 10 as well so it is not Linux related. Solution is to play the game in windowed mode. Locate: /home/YOURUSER/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/234630/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/Project CARS/graphicsconfigdx11.xml then you need to edit <prop name="Windowed" windowed="0" /> to <prop name="Windowed" windowed="1" />
I also adjusted Window Settings via KDE for this game to borderless. 
That's it. There could be a better easier solution.
title: Project CARS

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box. Done a few single races without graphical issues. No any problem with Alt+Tab. Playing on Logitech Gamepad F310.
title: Project CARS

notes: Force feedback is one of the best on this game (G920): you feel the car. Some crash from time to time after a few hours but nothing major.

title: Project CARS

notes: Nothing to tune to make it work. Performance is OK though not spectacular. Had to set my Logitech G29 to PS3 mode to make it work, but then everything's functional including force feedback.
title: Project CARS

notes:
With 4.2.9 the game runs almost perfect. Played about 13hours with not one crash. Only caveat i have is tabbing out of the game. When i do this the game will return a black screen, but this is no biggie. With 4.2.11 i got an blackscreen at start, which i did not analyze any futher.

My G920 Wheel is recognized perfectly and even the forcefeedback works. i need to mention that im using the feral udev rules (http://support.feralinteractive.com/docs/en/f12017/latest/linux/faqs/#i_steering_wheels_udev_rules).

I cant tell u much about Multiplayer. I could not test it meanwhile cause there are not many players anymore. :(
title: Project CARS
